title: Bridge Essay: Brain mechanisms controlling Pavlovian fear conditioning.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Brain, mechanisms, controlling, Pavlovian, fear.

**Brain mechanisms controlling Pavlovian fear conditioning.** — J Exp Psychol Anim Learn Cogn. PMID: 30407061. DOI: 10.1037/xan0000181. Akey insight of associative learning theory is that learning depends on the actions of prediction error: adiscrepancy between the actual and expected outcomes of aconditioning trial. This view of learning has inspired, and in turn been supported by, work in the neurosciences ranging from single unit recording and neuroimaging studies to pharmacological, chemogenetic, and optogenetic interventions.
